SEARCH

Package neqsim.process.allocation

package neqsim.process.allocation
Linear recovery-factor production allocation (proxy network).

This package allocates the metered production of a process flowsheet back to its individual sources (wells, templates, commingled feeds) using frozen per-unit per-component split factors and a linear proxy network. It is a fast, scalable alternative to per-source component tagging: a single rigorous base-case run with one common component slate is enough, after which any number of sources can be allocated by superposition.

Method

Every non-stream unit that has outlet streams is a network node; streams are the edges. For each node the base case fixes a split factor 

 f_u(s,k) = (molar flow of component k leaving outlet s) / (molar flow of component k entering u)

Holding these factors frozen, the per-component node throughput vector v contributed by a source obeys the linear balance (I - A_k) v = b, where A_k is the routing matrix built from the split factors and b injects the source feed at its entry node. Because every conservative unit has split factors that sum to one across its outlets, the spectral radius of A_k is below one and the system always has a unique, convergent solution — recycle and reflux loops included. Custody allocation is then alloc(j,s,k) = f_w(s,k) * v_w.

Scope

The approach applies to any conservative separation / scrubber / column / valve / cooler / heater / mixer / splitter / pump / compressor network, and therefore to all oil and gas field process configurations. Reactive or mass-transfer contacting units (amine, glycol, MEG, scavengers) are handled as black boxes that reproduce whatever redistribution the base case produced; for hydrocarbon allocation, water and MEG handling does not affect the result.

Key classes

  • SourceAllocator — high-level facade.
  • RecoveryFactorExtractor — builds the master component slate and frozen split factors from a solved base case.
  • AllocationNetwork — assembles the routing matrices and resolves source/custody streams.
  • LinearAllocationSolver — solves (I - A_k) v = b per component (direct solve with Neumann-series fallback).
  • ProductionAllocationResult — queryable result with unit conversion, product aggregation, mass closure and JSON export.
Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim Development Team
  • Related Packages
    Package
    Description
    neqsim.process
     
  • Class
    Description
    AllocationComparison
    Compares the results of several AllocationMethods run on the same base case, auto-evaluates how much they disagree, reports how each method would benefit each owner (source), and recommends a method.
    AllocationComparison.OwnerSensitivity
    One row of the owner-benefit table: a single source's allocation of a single product under each method, plus the spread across methods.
    AllocationMethod
    The family of production-allocation methods that MultiMethodAllocator can run and compare.
    AllocationNetwork
    Linear proxy network assembled from frozen per-unit split factors.
    AllocationSource
    A tagged production source (well, template, satellite feed, commingled inlet, ...) injected into the allocation proxy network.
    AllocationUncertaintyEstimator
    Closed-form first-order uncertainty propagation for the linear recovery-factor allocation network.
    AllocationUncertaintyEstimator.UncertaintyResult
    Result of an uncertainty-propagation run: per-source, per-custody, per-component variances of the allocated component flow plus aggregated totals.
    CustodyOutlet
    A tagged custody (sales / export / disposal) outlet stream that receives an allocated share of each production source.
    LinearAllocationSolver
    Solves the linear production-allocation network per component by superposition.
    LinearAllocationSolver.ComponentDiagnostics
    Diagnostics for the solve of a single component.
    LinearAllocationSolver.SolverResult
    Result of a network solve: per-component node inlet flows plus per-component diagnostics.
    MultiMethodAllocator
    Runs and compares the three production-allocation facility-split methods of AllocationMethod (component ratio, all-in linear proxy, and isolated stand-alone re-simulation) on a single rigorous base case.
    ProductionAllocationResult
    Result of a linear production-allocation run: how much of each source ends up in each custody outlet, resolved per component and aggregated to product groups and mass/mole totals.
    ProductType
    Custody product category used to aggregate per-component allocations into sales/export product groups (gas, oil/condensate, water).
    RecoveryFactorExtractor
    Extracts frozen per-unit per-component split (recovery) factors from a rigorous base-case ProcessSystem run.
    SourceAllocator
    High-level facade for linear recovery-factor production allocation.
    UnitSplit
    Frozen per-component split (recovery) factors for a single conservative process unit.
